What is going on here is the people who knew Saul beforehand are questioning this apparent spiritual conversion. The appellation "son of Kish" is a Semitism and is pejorative, suggesting the person is not noteworthy enough to be known by his own name. Thus, when they say "Is Saul also among the prophets", it is sarcastic, cp. v. 27. But, these sarcastic men are questioned by one more astute in v.…
